name: VidTide-M0
description: VidTide is an in-the-wild living benchmark for AI-generated video detection. M0 is the frozen submission slice (2026-04): 22,869 video records harvested from YouTube, Bilibili, Reddit, and official model showcase galleries, labelled by a three-tier provenance taxonomy (T1 official galleries; T2 platform AI-disclosure tags; T3 keyword + LLM verification). Each record carries the original platform URL; videos are recovered from the URL at evaluation time and are not redistributed (VidProM precedent). The benchmark is updated monthly; M0 is immutable.
citeAs: @misc{vidtide_m0_2026, title={{VidTide}: A Living In-the-Wild Benchmark for Measuring the Static-Benchmark Gap in AI-Generated Video Detection}, author={Anonymous}, year={2026}, note={NeurIPS 2026 Datasets and Benchmarks Track submission (under double-blind review). Anonymous code repository: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/vidtide-anon-C0B8/}}
rai:dataCollection: Continuous automated crawl of four source platforms (YouTube, Bilibili, Reddit, official generator showcase galleries). Each per-platform crawler uses public APIs or yt-dlp search; AI-generated candidates are surfaced via (T1) official-vendor showcase scrapers, (T2) platform-mandated AI-disclosure tags (YouTube AI-disclosure since 2024, Bilibili `argue_info` tag enforcing the China AI-labelling regulation since Sept 2025, TikTok C2PA), and (T3) title/description keyword match followed by GPT-4o-mini semantic verification. The full M0 frozen slice (22,869 records) was crawled between 2026-02 and 2026-04. The pipeline operates at approximately 156 USD per month in standard mode.
rai:dataAnnotation: Labels (real / fake) are not crowd-sourced; they are derived from platform-native AI-disclosure metadata and from official-gallery provenance. A subset (n=900) was independently re-annotated by three trained graduate research assistants for label-quality auditing; pairwise Cohen's kappa was 0.93 with a 0.6 percent clear-error rate. Annotators were compensated at standard institutional rates above local minimum wage.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Crawl candidates pass through (i) a quality filter (resolution / duration / fps thresholds), (ii) a tag-tier filter (T1 / T2 / T3 gating), (iii) GPT-4o-mini title/description verification (separating actual AI-generated clips from tutorials about AI), (iv) CLIP-based perceptual deduplication (ViT-B/32 embeddings, cosine threshold 0.92, prefer-higher-tier conflict resolution), and (v) a Cleanlab-based label-quality audit. Per-clip SHA-256 is computed at crawl time and stored for re-download integrity verification.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: M0 is the frozen submission slice; subsequent monthly slices (M1, M2, ...) will be appended as immutable additions and will not modify M0. A 24-hour opt-out flow is provided via the anonymous repository's issue tracker during double-blind review (and a persistent contact endpoint after acceptance); manifest deletion immediately revokes the clip from all reproduced slices. Errata are tracked additively in per-slice ERRATA.md files and never overwrite frozen records.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: Records contain only public platform metadata (platform-issued URL, platform-issued ID, uploader-supplied title and tags) plus crawl-time SHA-256 of the video bytes. No personally identifiable information is added by the dataset; uploader handles are not reproduced as separate fields. Faces in source videos are not extracted, augmented, or otherwise processed; the dataset is intended for detection-pipeline benchmarking, not biometric study.
rai:dataLimitations: (i) Tier-2 ground truth depends on platforms enforcing AI-disclosure correctly; we measure label noise at 0.6 percent clear errors. (ii) M0 is Bilibili-dominant (84.7 percent); YouTube (n=461) and Reddit (n=2,556) sub-pools constrain cross-platform claims. (iii) Generator identity is unknown for 81.5 percent of fake-labelled clips. (iv) The Kinetics-400 real subset (n=500, 3.9 percent) exhibits domain shift relative to platform-native real content. (v) Probe metadata (resolution, fps) is incomplete on early Bilibili crawls.
rai:dataBiases: Platform composition skew toward Bilibili reflects Chinese-language AI-video prevalence on that platform combined with the Sept-2025 China AI-labelling regulation enforcement. Generator distribution is heavy-tailed and dominated by Kling 2.x and other 2025--2026 frontier diffusion models; older GAN-era generators are under-represented and should not be evaluated against M0 in isolation. Topic distribution skews toward short-form entertainment content (vtuber, dance, anime stylisation), reflecting the consumer-facing AI-video deployment pattern at submission time.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Improved AI-video detection serves public trust by enabling defenders (platforms, fact-checkers, courts) to keep pace with generator evolution. Open evaluation of detector robustness benefits the defender community more than attackers, since attackers already operate adaptively against any deployed detector. The metadata-only release model avoids amplifying redistribution of synthetic media.
rai:dataUseCases: (i) Benchmarking AI-generated video detectors against in-the-wild content. (ii) Measuring the static-benchmark overestimation gap (lab benchmark AUROC minus in-the-wild AUROC). (iii) Studying generator-level and platform-level detection blind spots (including platform re-encoding confounds). (iv) Training data for fine-tuning standard video backbones for AI-generation detection.

distribution.name: github-repo
distribution.description: Anonymous code + manifest repository hosted via 4open.science.

distribution.name: metadata.jsonl
distribution.description: M0 frozen-slice manifest. One JSON object per video record; 22,869 lines total.

distribution.name: splits/test.jsonl
distribution.description: 5K-real / 5K-fake gap-evaluation slice (1,999 records as released; the 5K/5K balanced view is constructed by `scripts/compute_gap.py`).

recordSet.name: videos
recordSet.description: One record per video clip in the M0 frozen slice. Video bytes are not included; recover them from `source_url` via `scripts/download_videos.py`.

field.name: source_platform
field.description: Origin platform: youtube | bilibili | reddit | showcase.

field.name: source_url
field.description: Original platform URL (used at re-download time).

field.name: source_id
field.description: Platform-native ID (e.g., Bilibili BV ID, YouTube video ID).

field.name: tier_source
field.description: Provenance tier: tier1_official_showcase | tier2_platform_tag | tier2_channel_whitelist | tier3_keyword_llm.

field.name: claimed_generator
field.description: Declared generator identifier (e.g., kling21, sora2, veo3); null when not declared.

field.name: resolution_w
field.description: Width in pixels (null when probe metadata is unavailable).

field.name: resolution_h
field.description: Height in pixels (null when probe metadata is unavailable).

field.name: fps
field.description: Frame rate in frames per second (null when probe metadata is unavailable).

field.name: file_size_bytes
field.description: Video file size in bytes at original crawl time.

field.name: sha256
field.description: Content hash captured at original crawl time (used to detect platform re-encoding at re-download).

field.name: content_tags
field.description: JSON-encoded list of uploader-provided content tags.

field.name: published_at
field.description: Original platform publish timestamp (may be empty when not exposed by platform API).

field.name: crawled_at
field.description: Timestamp at which this record was added to the VidTide pipeline.
